sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Tx3 by TxPipe, an open API layer for Cardano dApp protocols.
Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, submitted by Intersect on behalf of TxPipe.

Deliverables: Open standardized interface across Cardano on-chain protocols, 12 additional protocol onboardings over 12 months, Developer and agent coverage for onboarded protocols, MCP server, Protocol skills framework, Agent-first documentation, Auto-generated SDKs in multiple languages, Live JSON-RPC endpoints

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Tx3 is intended to provide a shared interface layer that lets developers and AI agents discover, integrate, and compose Cardano protocols through documentation, SDKs, JSON-RPC endpoints, MCP tooling, and protocol skills.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal says there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild protocol understanding independently.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says TxPipe has already put the developer layer into production through Catalyst Fund 14 with 5 protocols live on the Tx3 Protocol Registry.
Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal commits to 12 additional protocol onboardings, scheduled as 3 per quarter, with full developer and agent coverage.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, medium materiality): The proposal states that Strike Finance, Indigo Protocol, VyFinance, Bodega Market, and Fluid Tokens are already accessible through Tx3.
missing: Independent evidence for: The proposal says there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild protocol understanding independently.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states TxPipe has received funding through 30 Project Catalyst proposals from Funds 9 through 14, with 26 delivered and 4 under development and on schedule, plus Intersect 2025 open-source maintenance funding for Pallas, Dolos, and UTxO RPC being delivered on schedule.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 1,308,000 ADA plus a 327,000 ADA contingency reserve over 12 months, with a total withdrawal of 1,684,050 ADA including a 49,050 ADA Intersect administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The proposal says there is currently no common interface mechanism across the Cardano ecosystem, forcing integrators to relearn each protocol and rebuild protocol understanding independently." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
